meta.note: Expanded manual seed + enhanced funding model (local + statewide). Scraper output in offices_up_*.json. Use for site data layer. All entries require agent + human accuracy review against official sources. Statewide rich data seeded in index.html; sync to JSON for maintainability.
meta.disclaimer: Informational only. Verify at sarasotavotes.gov, votemanatee.gov, and county finance portals.

districts.name: Florida Governor 2026 (Statewide - Local Impact on Growth & Marshlands)

incumbent.name: Open seat (Ron DeSantis term-limited)
incumbent.notes: Major impact on local development, taxes, environment, budget.

candidates.name: Byron Donalds (R frontrunner, Trump endorsed)
candidates.funding: ~$81M+ total raised (campaign + PACs as of mid-2026 reports)

candidates.name: James Fishback (R - Gen Z / grassroots challenger)
candidates.status: Crowd favorite, polling underdog but rising
candidates.funding: Lower than Donalds; relies on small donors and events

districts.fundingNotes: Statewide race with massive local stakes for Sarasota/Manatee growth and marshlands. Polling vs Reality: Mainstream polls show big Donalds lead. HOWEVER: Fishback's events and small-donor metrics suggest stronger support; HEAVY SKEPTICISM ON ALL POLLING.

districts.name: Florida Commissioner of Agriculture 2026 (Statewide - Marshlands & Ag vs Development)

incumbent.name: Wilton Simpson (R)
incumbent.notes: Incumbent; key player in ag land protection, hemp rules, and balancing development pressure on wetlands/marshlands.

topContributors.name: Florida Farm Bureau PAC and agribusiness groups (major in filings)

districts.fundingNotes: Critical local impact: FDACS policies on ag land preservation directly affect Sarasota/Manatee marshlands vs developer pressure. Heavy focus for marshlands transparency.
